lines.line: # Journey to America

> Beckford discusses his migration from Jamaica to the United States in 1951 due to labor shortages in Connecticut's tobacco industry, detailing the recruitment process, medical examinations, and initial cultural and logistical adjustments.
** migration, Jamaica, United States, 1951, labor shortage, recruitment, medical examinations, tobacco industry **
keywords: ** migration, Jamaica, United States, 1951, labor shortage, recruitment, medical examinations, tobacco industry **

story.title: Ozzie Beckford Oral History Part 1
story.description: The interview features Oswald Newton Beckford recounting his journey from Jamaica to the United States in 1951 for work in the tobacco industry due to a labor shortage. He describes the exhaustive pre-departure medical examinations and the detailed immigration process. Beckford discusses his work experiences in various states, friendships formed, adjustments to different living conditions, and his pursuit of mechanical skills. Personal triumphs include meeting his future wife, Gloria, adapting to American life, and establishing a home in Connecticut. The narrative encapsulates themes of immigration, labor, personal growth, and resilience amidst challenges.
